title: toolbox
text: package generalised container variety front-end function execute complex workflows embedded docker container. package includes set core functions required allow front-end function work different environments like MAC OS, Windows Linux. structure front-end shown figure1  package contains also simple skeleton function can used template creation new function. elements modified skeleton shown figure 2,  Panel show skeleton panel B modifications made ribomito_qc function. IMPORTANT: input-dir_path sketelon function must removed! refer folder data located computer folder mount /scratch docker container. , instead can find header R script present repbioinfo/ribomito docker container. can see parameters provided front-end function passed script script take care executing code requested analysis.
code: #!/usr/bin/env Rscript    library(data.table)   library(Matrix)   library(ggplot2)  # versione 3.1 mat_dgC <- as(mat_dgC, "dgCMatrix") was changed in mat_dgC <- as(mat_dgC, "CsparseMatrix"), since dgCMatrix is deprecated # expression binning was inserted in the in the output file ... #Version 3.6 support visium HD spatial transcriptomics output: embedding bc2cellid function, see below  setwd("/scratch") args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE) if (length(args) < 1) {   stop("Usage: Rscript Ribomito_v3.6.2.R <infile> <rownames_format> <kit> [beads_location] [counts_threshold] [out_metrics] [out_freq] [mtx_file] [barcodes_file] [features_file]") }  infile <- ifelse(length(args) >= 1, args[1]) rownames_format <- ifelse(length(args) >= 2, args[2], "symbol") kit <- ifelse(length(args) >= 3, args[3], "scRNAseq") beads_location <- if (length(args) >= 4) args[4] else NULL counts_threshold <- ifelse(length(args) >= 5, as.numeric(args[5]), 2) out_metrics <- ifelse(length(args) >= 6, args[6], "ribomito_metrics.csv") out_freq <- ifelse(length(args) >= 7, args[7], "ribomito_freq.csv") mtx_file <- if (length(args) >= 8) args[8] else NULL barcodes_file <- if (length(args) >= 9) args[9] else NULL features_file <- if (length(args) >= 10) args[10] else NULL  #' ribomito_qc #' #' Compute per-cell QC metrics: N.genes (counts > 2), ribo.score (%), mito.score (%) #' Supports: ... ribomito_qc <- function(infile,                             rownames_format = c("ensemblID:symbol", "symbol"),                               kit = c("scRNAseq", "scMultiomics10X", "Seeker", "visiumHD"),                               beads_location = NULL,                             counts_threshold = 2,                 out_metrics = "ribomito_metrics.csv",                 out_freq = "ribomito_Ngenes_freq.csv",                 mtx_file = NULL,                 barcodes_file = NULL,                 features_file = NULL) { ... } ribomito_qc(infile=infile, rownames_format=rownames_format, kit=kit, beads_location=beads_location, out_metrics=out_metrics, out_freq=out_freq,      mtx_file=mtx_file, barcodes_file=barcodes_file, features_file=features_file, counts_threshold=counts_threshold)

title: toolbox
text: function evolution mitoRiboUmi function available [rCASC package]. current implementation extends functionality original function sparse matrices provides additional QC information several single-cell platforms, including: 10x Genomics/Illumina single-cell RNA-seq (dense sparse matrices), 10x Genomics single-nucleus RNA-seq (sparse matrices), Takara Seeker spatial transcriptomics (dense comma-separated matrices), 10x Genomics Visium HD (sparse matrices). Support latter still development requires refinement. example dataset testing ribomitoQC available [Zenodo]. Figure 3, shows output scRNA-seq data generated 10x Genomics/Illumina single-cell RNA-seq platform. Panel reports percentage counts associated mitochondrial proteins plotted percentage counts associated ribosomal proteins. dot represents cell colored according number detected genes. case, used threshold defined [rCASC paper] paper, 2 UMIs required gene considered detected. Panel B shows frequency distribution cells across ranges detected genes. Panel C provides data used generate plot shown Panel . Figure 4 shows output single-nucleus RNA-seq data generated 10x Genomics platform. Panel shows MALAT1 long non-coding RNA expression plotted percentage mitochondrial gene counts. MALAT1 appears marker good cell quality associated low mitochondrial contamination. Panel B reports percentage counts associated mitochondrial proteins plotted percentage associated ribosomal proteins. dot represents cell colored according number detected genes. Also case, used threshold defined [rCASC paper] paper, 2 UMIs required call gene detected. Panel C shows frequency distribution cells across ranges detected genes. Panel D provides data used generate plots shown Panels B. Figure 5 shows output Takara Seeker spatial transcriptomics data.  Panel reports percentage counts associated mitochondrial proteins plotted percentage associated ribosomal proteins. dot represents cell colored according number detected genes. , used threshold defined rCASC paper paper, 2 UMIs required gene considered detected. Panel B shows data mapped according tissue location. Panel C shows frequency distribution cells across ranges detected genes. Panel D provides data used generate plots shown Panels B. generate output, input must also include file describing spatial location barcode.

title: toolbox
text: function front end Combat-ref published Zhang 2024. Thsi batch effect correction method enhances statistical power reliability differential expression analysis RNA-seq data. built foundations ComBat-seq, employs negative binomial model adjust count data innovates using pooled dispersion parameter entire batches preserving count data reference batch. figure 6, shown structure input files. panel shown tab separated count table panel B metadata saved comma separated file. Please column names counts tabla rownames metadata table must co-linear. Figure 7 shown effect Combat-ref adjustment GSE119004 dataset. dataset generated Shi et al.. Specifically provide RNAseq PDX, organoids patient RNA. Panel shows batches referred uncorrected samples. Panel B show data batch correction. notable PDXs organoids better aggregate corresponding patient batch correction. semplicity patient 274 shown

what: Check and Identify the Container Runtime — detect_container_runtime
title: Check and Identify the Container Runtime — detect_container_runtime
text: detect_container_runtime function checks current R process running  inside Docker Singularity container. returns name container runtime (either "Docker", "Singularity", "None") along boolean indicating whether running inside containers.

title: Check and Identify the Container Runtime — detect_container_runtime
Check and Identify the Container Runtime — detect_container_runtime
text: list two elements: name character string indicating container runtime: "Docker", "Singularity", "None". is_running logical value: TRUE process running inside recognized container runtime, FALSE otherwise.

title: Check and Identify the Container Runtime — detect_container_runtime
code: result <- detect_container_runtime() cat("Container runtime:", result$name, "\n") #> Container runtime: None  cat("Is running in container:", result$is_running, "\n") #> Is running in container: FALSE

title: Check if Docker is Available and Return Its Path — has_docker
Check if Docker is Available and Return Its Path — has_docker
text: list two elements: found logical value: TRUE Docker available system's PATH, FALSE otherwise. path character string containing full path Docker executable found,  empty string found.

title: Check if Docker is Available and Return Its Path — has_docker
code: result <- has_docker() if (result$found) {   cat("Docker is available at:", result$path, "\n") } else {   cat("Docker is not available.\n") } #> Docker is available at: /usr/bin/docker

title: ribomitoQC — ribomitoQC
text: function perform set QC single cell RNAseq, single cell nuclear RNAseq, Takara Seeker dense matrix, visium HD. returns statistics data csv format well plots QC plots.

title: ribomitoQC — ribomitoQC
code: ribomitoQC(   input_dir_path,   infile,   rownames_format,   kit,   beads_location = NULL,   counts_threshold = NULL,   out_metrics = NULL,   out_freq = NULL,   mtx_file = NULL,   barcodes_file = NULL,   features_file = NULL )

title: ribomitoQC — ribomitoQC
text: input_dir_path, character string indicating path directory containing data used infile Path dense counts file MatrixMarket .mtx file directory containing matrix.mtx / barcodes.tsv / features.tsv rownames_format One c("ensemblID:symbol","symbol"), symbol selected sparse matrix ensemblID:symbol default dense matrix rCASC output kit One c("scRNAseq", "scMultiomics10X", "Seeker", "visiumHD"). Note: scRNAseq works Illumina, 10Xgenomics sparse rCASC dense matrix beads_location comma separated file contains beads locations required Seeker specific visualisation. visiumHD  binary visium parquet requires parquet-tools installed system. Running parquet-tools generates comma separate file visium_parquet, contains association among barcodes different resolution 0.02, 0.08 0.16 um cellID counts_threshold Threshold "gene present" (default 2, used rCASC ribomito function) out_metrics Output CSV per-cell metrics out_freq Output CSV N.genes frequency bins mtx_file Optional explicit path matrix.mtx (overrides inference) barcodes_file Optional explicit path barcodes.tsv (overrides inference) features_file Optional explicit path features.tsv (overrides inference)

Supported organisms : 'Homo sapiens', 'Mus musculus' 'Drosophila melanogaster'

title: Execute code inside a scratch directory context. — with_scratch

title: Execute code inside a scratch directory context. — with_scratch
